strategic_profile.executive_summary: Drexel University is a private, R1 research institution in Philadelphia renowned for its rigorous cooperative education (co-op) program. The university operates on a fast-paced quarter system, allowing students to integrate up to 18 months of full-time, paid professional work experience into their undergraduate curriculum before graduation.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Drexel utilizes an intensive 10-week quarter system to accommodate the co-op scheduling.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students must be prepared for a fast-paced, rigorous academic environment with less downtime than traditional semester systems.

analyst_takeaways.insight: While the total cost of attendance exceeds $83,000 annually, average institutional aid is highly generous, with 100% of incoming first-years receiving some form of support.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The sticker price is high, but the net price is significantly lower for most students, further offset by paid co-op earnings averaging over $22,000 per 6-month cycle.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The overall acceptance rate is highly accessible at 79.4%, but yields are low (around 8-9%).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Drexel utilizes admissions to build a large funnel of career-driven students, though specific pathways (like the BS/MD Early Assurance program) remain highly competitive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus culture is intensely pre-professional, driven by the co-op search and industry integration.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students who thrive here are self-motivated, pragmatic, and heavily focused on postgraduate career placement.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Urban, fast-paced environment where social life is largely influenced by the shifting presence of students alternating between academic terms and full-time co-op positions.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Deeply integrated into Philadelphia's University City; extensive public transit access facilitates seamless commuting to co-op jobs across the metro area.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution, Drexel offers substantial undergraduate research opportunities, especially within STEM fields, supported by the Drexel Solutions Institute and major federal grants.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for most programs; test scores are considered if submitted. Applicants to the BA/BS+MD Early Assurance program are required to submit standardized test scores.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Yield rate has seen a decline.

gpa.notes: Average GPA reflects high academic readiness among enrolled students.

colleges.name: College of Computing & Informatics
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking rapid integration into the tech industry through co-ops.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Internal transfers must attain an 'A' in CS 164 OR a 'B-' in CS 171 OR a 'C' in CS 172, along with a 'C' or better in MATH 121 to switch into this major.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students looking to balance rigorous foundational engineering coursework with hands-on application.

majors.name: Biomedical Engineering
majors.notes: Physics is strongly recommended but not required.

supplemental_requirements.details: 1 essay (~500 words) reflecting on experiences, personal characteristics, and unique traits that prepare the student for the major.
1 essay (~500 words). Test scores are required.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Drexel actively evaluates demonstrated interest; students should interact with admissions events to improve yield probabilities.

major_selection_tactics: Applying Early Action heavily increases the statistical likelihood of admission, approaching a 96% acceptance rate.
Most majors do not require supplemental essays, decreasing application friction for undeclared or general admission candidates.

Internal transfers into competitive fields like Computer Science are permitted, providing an alternate pathway if initial admission to the major isn't possible, provided strict GPA prerequisites are met.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Students can enroll in less impacted majors within the College of Computing & Informatics (like Information Systems) and execute an internal transfer to Computer Science by completing the first quarter with a 2.5 GPA and strong grades in foundational Math/CS courses.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate structure where students do not pay tuition during their full-time co-op terms, paying only University fees.
financials.aid_philosophy: Need-Aware / Does not meet 100% of demonstrated need

scholarships.name: Drexel Liberty Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Covers 100% tuition for 65 academically talented Philadelphia high school students from low-income families.

scholarships.name: Drexel Global Scholar
scholarships.benefits: Full tuition for exceptional international students.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Merit scholarship renewable for four years.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for scores of 4 or 5 on most AP exams.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to bypass prerequisites or fulfill elective credits, helping students cope with the rigorous 10-week terms.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for Higher Level (HL) subjects with scores of 5 or higher. Standard Level (SL) subjects are not awarded credit.

tools: Direct articulation agreements with regional community colleges
transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer credits must map to equivalent courses in the desired curriculum and require a grade of C (2.0) or better.

what_it_takes: Strong time-management skills to survive the extremely fast-paced 10-week quarter system.
Career-focused pragmatism to succeed in the alternating study and co-op cycles.
High GPA and demonstrated rigor in high school coursework (AP/IB) to bypass introductory weeds.
essay_tips: For specific majors, directly connect past experiences to real-world career goals.
Show how you will utilize the co-op program to bridge the gap between academic theory and industry application.
red_flags: Expecting a traditional relaxed 'college experience'—the quarter system is notoriously intense.
Struggling with bureaucracy ('The Drexel Shaft') when navigating financial aid and co-op placements.
